name: AgGrid

unions.name: Aggregation
unions.comment: Variants to aggregate values for a grouped column.

unions.name: CellEditor
unions.comment:
CellEditor

  - `DefaultEditor` will set an editor derived from the renderer
  - `PredefinedEditor` allows to use an existing ag-grid editor
  - `AppEditor` allows to use an Elm app as custom editor

unions.name: EventType
unions.comment: Possible variants of callbacks that can lead to a certain change.

unions.name: FilterState
unions.comment:
Filter state.

Can be used to evaluate the current filter configuration whenever a filter on a column gets changed.

Might be used to persist the filters applied to the table for the users to the localstorage or some
other external storage. Filter states can be restored through the `filterState` on the `GridConfig`.

AG Grid reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/filtering/#column-filter-types>

unions.name: LockPosition
unions.comment:
Lock a column to position to left or right to always have this column displayed in that position.

Or can be configured to not lock in one position and move the column freely.

unions.name: PinningType
unions.comment: Possible options to pin a column.

unions.name: Renderer
unions.comment:
The `Renderer` expresses how the data is retrieved from the list of data
and rendered to the view.

The editor and renderer views in the table depend on the type.
For example, a `BoolRenderer` is displayed and can be edited with a checkbox
in the table.

The `Renderer` may need a function to read the value for the cell from the
records.


### Example

If the data is list of `Product`, the `Renderer` to display the column for
the `title` might be defined as:

    type alias Product =
        { title : String }

    -- Column definitions:
    [{ renderer = StringRenderer .title, ... }]

unions.name: RowGroupPanelVisibility
unions.comment: Row Group Panel allows the users to control which columns the rows are grouped by.

unions.name: RowSelection
unions.comment:
Type of row selection, set to either `SingleRowSelection` or `MultipleRowSelection` to enable selection.

  - `SingleRowSelection` will use single row selection, such that when you select a row, any previously selected row gets unselected.
  - `MultipleRowSelection` allows multiple rows to be selected.
  - `NoRowSelection` will not perform a row selection, only selecting the cell.

aliases.name: ColumnDef
aliases.comment:
Column definition.

Requires `field`, `renderer`, `headerName`, and `settings` to be defined
for each column.

  - `field`: name of the column and specifically used to decode data
    properly (e.g. from events)

  - `renderer`: used to render data to a cell (for more information see
    the documentation for `Renderer`)

  - `headerName`: displayed text in the column header

  - `settings`: allows to customize each column - a `defaultSettings`
    record is offered, which can be adjusted to your own needs to reduce
    the implemenation overhead.


### Example

    let
        defaultSettings =
            AgGrid.defaultSettings
    in
    [ { field = "id"
      , renderer = IntRenderer .id
      , headerName = "Id"
      , settings = defaultSettings
      }
    , { field = "title"
      , renderer = StringRenderer .title
      , headerName = "Product"
      , settings =
            { defaultSettings
                | editable = False
                , pinned = AgGrid.PinnedToLeft
            }
      }
    ]
aliases.type: { field : String.String, renderer : AgGrid.Renderer dataType, headerName : String.String, settings : AgGrid.ColumnSettings }

aliases.name: ColumnSettings
aliases.type: { aggFunc : AgGrid.Aggregation, allowedAggFuncs : Maybe.Maybe (List.List AgGrid.Aggregation), defaultAggFunc : AgGrid.Aggregation, autoHeaderHeight : Basics.Bool, cellClassRules : List.List AgGrid.ClassRule, checkboxSelection : Basics.Bool, customCellEditor : AgGrid.CellEditor, editable : AgGrid.Expression.Eval Basics.Bool, enablePivot : Basics.Bool, enableRowGroup : Basics.Bool, enableValue : Basics.Bool, filter : AgGrid.FilterType, filterParams : { buttons : List.List AgGrid.FilterButtonType, closeOnApply : Basics.Bool }, filterValueGetter : Maybe.Maybe String.String, flex : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, floatingFilter : Basics.Bool, headerCheckboxSelection : Basics.Bool, hide : Basics.Bool, lockPosition : AgGrid.LockPosition, minWidth : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, pinned : AgGrid.PinningType, pivot : Basics.Bool, pivotIndex : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, resizable : Basics.Bool, rowGroup : Basics.Bool, rowGroupIndex : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, showDisabledCheckboxes : Basics.Bool, sortable : Basics.Bool, sort : AgGrid.Sorting, sortIndex : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, suppressColumnsToolPanel : Basics.Bool, suppressFiltersToolPanel : Basics.Bool, suppressMenu : Basics.Bool, suppressSizeToFit : Basics.Bool, valueFormatter : Maybe.Maybe String.String, valueGetter : Maybe.Maybe String.String, valueParser : Maybe.Maybe String.String, valueSetter : Maybe.Maybe String.String, width : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Float, wrapHeaderText : Basics.Bool }

aliases.name: ColumnState
aliases.comment:
Column state.

Can be used to evaluate the current table state whenever a change to the structure of the table
happens (e.g. sorting, order, sizing, or visiblity of columns changed).

Might be used to persist the table state of the user to the localstorage or some other external
storage. Column states can be restored through the `columnState` on the `GridConfig`.
aliases.type: { aggFunc : Maybe.Maybe String.String, colId : String.String, flex : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, hide : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Bool, pinned : Maybe.Maybe String.String, pivot : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Bool, pivotIndex : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, rowGroup : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Bool, rowGroupIndex : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, sort : Maybe.Maybe String.String, sortIndex : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, width : Basics.Float }

aliases.name: CsvExportParams
aliases.comment: Possible configuration for the CSV export.

aliases.name: ExcelExportParams
aliases.comment: Possible configuration for the Excel export.

aliases.name: GridConfig
aliases.type: { autoGroupColumnDef : { cellRendererParams : { suppressCount : Basics.Bool, checkbox : Basics.Bool }, headerName : Maybe.Maybe String.String, minWidth : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, resizable : Basics.Bool }, autoSizeColumns : Basics.Bool, cacheQuickFilter : Basics.Bool, columnStates : List.List AgGrid.ColumnState, contextMenu : Maybe.Maybe AgGrid.ContextMenu.ContextMenu, csvExport : Maybe.Maybe AgGrid.CsvExportParams, detailRenderer : Maybe.Maybe { componentName : String.String, componentParams : Maybe.Maybe Json.Encode.Value, rowHeight : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int }, disableResizeOnScroll : Basics.Bool, excelExport : Maybe.Maybe AgGrid.ExcelExportParams, filterStates : Dict.Dict String.String AgGrid.FilterState, groupDefaultExpanded : Basics.Int, groupIncludeFooter : Basics.Bool, groupIncludeTotalFooter : Basics.Bool, groupSelectsChildren : Basics.Bool, isRowSelectable : dataType -> Basics.Bool, maintainColumnOrder : Basics.Bool, pagination : Basics.Bool, quickFilterText : String.String, rowGroupPanelShow : AgGrid.RowGroupPanelVisibility, rowHeight : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, rowId : Maybe.Maybe (dataType -> String.String), rowMultiSelectWithClick : Basics.Bool, rowSelection : AgGrid.RowSelection, rowClassRules : List.List AgGrid.ClassRule, selectedIds : List.List String.String, sideBar : AgGrid.Sidebar, size : String.String, sizeToFitAfterFirstDataRendered : Basics.Bool, stopEditingWhenCellsLoseFocus : Basics.Bool, suppressMenuHide : Basics.Bool, suppressRowClickSelection : Basics.Bool, suppressRowDeselection : Basics.Bool, themeClasses : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

aliases.name: Sidebar
aliases.type: { panels : List.List AgGrid.SidebarType, defaultToolPanel : Maybe.Maybe AgGrid.SidebarType, position : AgGrid.SidebarPosition }

aliases.name: StateChange
aliases.comment: Change event data for a table state changes.

values.name: aggregationToString
values.comment:
Stringify an Aggregation.

This matches the configuration for AgGrid and equals the value retrieved from the `ColumnState`.
Further, it can be used for external communication if necessary.

values.name: applyColumnState
values.comment:
Apply the column state from the `GridConfig` to the given `ColumnDefs`.

The values from the cache overwrite the values on the ColumnDef. The order is also according
to the order in the column state. New columns, that don't exist in the column state, are appended to the end.

**This function is mainly exposed to allow unit-testing, as this is automatically applied to ColumnDefs passed to the `grid`.**
values.type: AgGrid.GridConfig dataType -> List.List (AgGrid.ColumnDef dataType) -> List.List (AgGrid.ColumnDef dataType)

values.name: columnStatesDecoder
values.comment:
Decoder for the current column states.

Can be used to retrieve and decode the column states from an external storage.

values.name: columnStatesEncoder
values.comment:
Encode a collection of `ColumnState`.

Can be used to persist the current list of `ColumnState` for the grid to an external storage.

values.name: defaultGridConfig
values.comment:
Retrieve a `GridConfig` record with default configuration.

Can be used when implementing the grid.

        { autoGroupColumnDef =
            { cellRendererParams =
                { suppressCount = False
                , checkbox = False
                }
            , headerName = Nothing
            , minWidth = Nothing
            , resizable = True
            }
        , autoSizeColumns = False
        , cacheQuickFilter = False
        , columnStates = []
        , detailRenderer = Nothing
        , disableResizeOnScroll = False
        , filterStates = Dict.empty
        , groupDefaultExpanded = 0
        , groupIncludeFooter = False
        , groupIncludeTotalFooter = False
        , groupSelectsChildren = False
        , isRowSelectable = always True
        , pagination = False
        , quickFilterText = ""
        , rowGroupPanelShow = NeverVisible
        , rowHeight = Nothing
        , rowMultiSelectWithClick = False
        , rowSelection = MultipleRowSelection
        , selectedIds = []
        , sideBar = defaultSidebar
        , size = "65vh"
        , sizeToFitAfterFirstDataRendered = True
        , stopEditingWhenCellsLoseFocus = True
        , suppressMenuHide = False
        , suppressRowClickSelection = False
        , suppressRowDeselection = False
        , themeClasses = Nothing
        }

values.name: defaultSettings
values.comment:
Retrieve a `ColumnSettings` record with default configuration.

Can be used when implementing column configurations for the table.

Default column settings:

    { aggFunc = NoAggregation
    , allowedAggFuncs = Nothing
    , defaultAggFunc = SumAggregation
    , autoHeaderHeight = False
    , cellClassRules = []
    , checkboxSelection = False
    , customCellEditor = DefaultEditor
    , editable = Const False
    , enablePivot = True
    , enableRowGroup = True
    , enableValue = True
    , filter = DefaultFilter
    , filterParams =
        { buttons = [ ClearButton ]
        , closeOnApply = False
        }
    , filterValueGetter = Nothing
    , flex = Nothing
    , floatingFilter = False
    , headerCheckboxSelection = False
    , hide = False
    , lockPosition = NoPositionLock
    , minWidth = Nothing
    , pinned = Unpinned
    , pivot = False
    , pivotIndex = Nothing
    , resizable = True
    , rowGroup = False
    , rowGroupIndex = Nothing
    , showDisabledCheckboxes = False
    , sortable = True
    , sort = NoSorting
    , sortIndex = Nothing
    , suppressColumnsToolPanel = False
    , suppressFiltersToolPanel = False
    , suppressMenu = False
    , suppressSizeToFit = True
    , valueFormatter = Nothing
    , valueGetter = Nothing
    , valueParser = Nothing
    , valueSetter = Nothing
    , width = Nothing
    , wrapHeaderText = False
    }

values.name: defaultSidebar
values.comment:
Retrieve a `Sidebar` with default configuration.

Can be used to ease the sidebar configuration.

        { panels = []
        , defaultToolPanel = Nothing
        , position = SidebarRight
        }

values.name: filterStatesDecoder
values.comment:
Decoder for the filter states.

Can be used to retrieve and decode the filter states from an external storage.

values.name: filterStatesEncoder
values.comment:
Encode a collection of `FilterState`.

Can be used to persist the current collection of `FilterState` for the grid to an external storage.

values.name: grid
values.comment:
Defines the data grid.


## Example

    let
        gridConfig =
            AgGrid.defaultGridConfig
                |> (\config -> { config | themeClasses = Just "ag-theme-balham ag-basic" })

        events =
            [ onCellChanged rowDecoder UpdateProduct ]

        defaultSettings =
            AgGrid.defaultSettings
                |> (\settings -> { settings | editable = True })

        columns =
            [ { field = "id"
              , renderer = IntRenderer .id
              , headerName = "Id"
              , settings = { defaultSettings | enablePivot = False }
              }
            , { field = "title"
              , renderer = StringRenderer .title
              , headerName = "Product"
              , settings = { defaultSettings | filter = StringFilter }
              }
            ]

        data =
            model.products
    in
    grid gridConfig events columns data
values.type: AgGrid.GridConfig dataType -> List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> List.List (AgGrid.ColumnDef dataType) -> List.List dataType -> Html.Html msg

values.name: onCellChanged
values.comment:
Detect change events on cells.

Decodes the changed row according to the provided `dataDecoder` and passes the result to
the message `toMsg`.

Handles the `cellValueChanged` event from Ag Grid.
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder dataType -> (Result.Result Json.Decode.Error dataType -> msg) -> Html.Attribute msg

values.name: onCellClicked
values.comment:
Detect click events on cells.

Decodes the row of the clicked cell according to the provided `dataDecoder` and the field
name of the clicked cells as tuple and passes the result to the message `toMsg`.

Handles the `cellClicked` event from Ag Grid.
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder dataType -> (( Result.Result Json.Decode.Error dataType, String.String ) -> msg) -> Html.Attribute msg

values.name: onCellDoubleClicked
values.comment:
Detect doubleclick events on cells.

Decodes the row of the clicked cell according to the provided `dataDecoder` and the field
name of the clicked cells as tuple and passes the result to the message `toMsg`.

Handles the `cellDoubleClicked` event from Ag Grid.
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder dataType -> (( Result.Result Json.Decode.Error dataType, String.String ) -> msg) -> Html.Attribute msg

values.name: onColumnStateChanged
values.comment:
Detect change events to the table structure (e.g. sorting or moved columns).

Decodes the `EventType` of the change and the current states of the grid columns and passes
the result to the `toMsg`.

values.name: onContextMenu
values.comment: Detect click on custom context menu actions
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder dataType -> (( Result.Result Json.Decode.Error dataType, String.String ) -> msg) -> Html.Attribute msg

values.name: onFilterStateChanged
values.comment:
Detect change events to the table filters.

Decodes the `EventType` of the change and the filter configuration of the grid columns and passes
the result to the `toMsg`.

values.name: onSelectionChange
values.comment:
Detect selection change events.

Sends the current selection of [nodes](https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/row-object/) to the `toMsg`.
values.type: Json.Decode.Decoder node -> (Result.Result Json.Decode.Error (List.List node) -> msg) -> Html.Attribute msg

values.name: pinningTypeToString
values.comment:
Stringify a PinningType.

This matches the configuration for AgGrid and equals the value retrieved from the `ColumnState`.
Further, it can be used for external communication if necessary.

values.name: sortingToString
values.comment:
Stringify the Sorting.

This matches the configuration for AgGrid and equals the value retrieved from the `ColumnState`.
Further, it can be used for external communication if necessary.

values.name: toAggregation
values.comment:
Parse a string to an Aggregation.

This can be used in combination with the value on the `ColumnState`.
Further, it can be used for external communication if necessary.

values.name: toPinningType
values.comment:
Parse a string to a PinningType.

This can be used in combination with the value on the `ColumnState`.
Further, it can be used for external communication if necessary.

values.name: toSorting
values.comment:
Parse a string to the Sorting type.

This can be used in combination with the value on the `ColumnState`.
Further, it can be used for external communication if necessary.

name: AgGrid.ContextMenu

aliases.name: ContextMenu
aliases.comment: Type alias for a context menu.

values.name: autoSizeAllContextAction
values.comment:
Auto-size all columns.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: chartRangeContextAction
values.comment:
Chart a range of selected cells. Only shown if charting is enabled.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: contextAction
values.comment: Create a custom context action
values.type: { name : String.String, checked : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Bool, actionName : Maybe.Maybe String.String, disabled : AgGrid.Expression.Eval Basics.Bool, icon : Maybe.Maybe String.String, cssClasses : List.List ( List.List String.String, AgGrid.Expression.Eval Basics.Bool ), subMenu : List.List AgGrid.ContextMenu.ContextAction } -> AgGrid.ContextMenu.ContextAction

values.name: contextSeparator
values.comment:
Adds a septerator to the context menu.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: contractAllContextAction
values.comment:
Collapse all groups. When set, it's only shown if grouping by at least one column.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: copyContextAction
values.comment:
Copy selected value to clipboard.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: copyWithGroupHeadersContextAction
values.comment:
Copy selected value to clipboard with headers and header groups.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: copyWithHeadersContextAction
values.comment:
Copy selected value to clipboard with headers.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: csvExportContextAction
values.comment:
Export to CSV using all default export values.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: cutContextAction
values.comment:
Cut the selected value to clipboard.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: defaultActionAttributes
values.comment:
Retrieves `ContextActionAttributes` with default configuration.

Can be used to ease the context action attributes configuration.

        { name = ""
        , checked = Nothing
        , disabled = Const False
        , actionName = Nothing
        , icon = Nothing
        , subMenu = []
        }

values.name: encode
values.comment: Encodes the ContextMenu type to json.

values.name: excelExportContextAction
values.comment:
Export to Excel (.xlsx) using all default export values.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: expandAllContextAction
values.comment:
When set, it's only shown if grouping by at least one column.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: exportContextAction
values.comment:
Export sub menu (containing csvExport and excelExport).

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: pasteContextAction
values.comment:
Always disabled.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: pivotChartContextAction
values.comment:
Chart all grouped and pivoted data from the grid. Only shown if charting is enabled and in Pivot Mode.

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

values.name: resetColumnsContextAction
values.comment:
Reset all columns

Reference: <https://www.ag-grid.com/javascript-data-grid/context-menu/#built-in-menu-items>

name: AgGrid.Expression

unions.name: Eval
unions.comment:
`Eval a` can be a `Cosnt a` or and `Expr`

    A `Const a` will pass the value of the type `a` directly to the `AgGrid` attribute in javascript.
    A `Expr` will frist evaluated in javascript and than passed to the `AgGrid` attribute.

unions.name: Expression
unions.comment:
A `Expression` covers some basic operators to evaluate

At this moment this only covers very basic operations

unions.name: Literal
unions.comment: The `Literal` type represents literals for strings, ints and floats.

unions.name: Operator
unions.comment: A `Operator` take expression(s) and evaluate the result in javascript.

values.name: and
values.comment:
Represents the && operator

    Expression.and (Expression.value "likesElm") (Expression.value "isProgrammer")

values.name: bool
values.comment:
A bool literal

    Expression.bool True

values.name: encode
values.comment: The encode encodes a evaluation.

values.name: eq
values.comment:
Represents the == operator

    Expression.eq (Expression.int 18) (Expression.value "age")

values.name: float
values.comment:
A float Literal

    Expression.float 13.37

values.name: gte
values.comment:
Represents the >= operator

    Expression.gte (Expression.int 18) (Expression.value "age")

values.name: includes
values.comment:
Performs the javascript Array.inclues function

    Expression.includes (Expression.string "Tim") (Expression.value "names")
    Where names is a list of Strings.

values.name: int
values.comment:
An int Literal

    Expression.int 18

values.name: list
values.comment:
A string-list literal

    Expression.list [ "a", "b" ]

values.name: lte
values.comment:
Represents the <= operator

    Expression.lte (Expression.int 18) (Expression.value "age")

values.name: not
values.comment:
Represents the ! operator

    Expression.not (Expression.value "isActive")

values.name: or
values.comment:
Represents the || operator

    Expression.or (Expression.value "likesElm") (Expression.value "isProgrammer")

values.name: string
values.comment:
A string Literal

    Expression.string "Tim"

values.name: value
values.comment:
The value function can be used to get a value from the data object passed in from a callback of `AgGrid`.

For example when it comes to disable a contextMenu entry, you can get a value from the current clicked row.

    type alias Row =
        { age : Int }

    columnDefs =
        [ { field = "age"
          , renderer = IntRenderer .age
          , headerName = "Age"
          , settings = AgGrid.defaultSettings
          }
        ]

    contextMenu =
        [ AgGridContextMenu.contextAction
            { defaultActionAttributes
                | name = "Full age action"
                , actionName = Just "fullAgeAction"
                , disabled = Expression.Expr (Expression.lte (Expression.value "age") (Expression.int 18))
            }
        ]

name: AgGrid.ValueFormat

values.name: booleanFilterValueGetter
values.comment:
Format a boolean cell value with a proper translation.

This is similar to using a normal Value Getter, but is specific to the filter.

    cellValue = true

    booleanFilterValueGetter "boolField"
    > "Yes"
values.type: { true : String.String, false : String.String, field : String.String } -> String.String

values.name: currencyValueFormatter
dateFilterValueGetter
decimalValueFormatter
numberFilterValueGetter
values.comment:
Format a cell value as number.

This is similar to using a normal Value Getter, but is specific to the filter.

    cellValue = "15"

    numberFilterValueGetter "numberField"
    > 15

values.name: numberValueGetter
values.comment:
Casts the field value as a number.

This is important if the cell value is given as a string (e.g. for currencies) but needs to a number
in order to aggregate the values.

values.name: percentValueFormatter
values.comment:
Format a cell value as localized PERCENT value utilizing the Javascript `Intl.NumberFormat` class.

    cellValue = 0.15

    percentValueFormatter { countryCode = "de-DE" }
    > "15%"
